title: planning-spine: test run writes tracked generated artifacts and they drift from source
description:
Discovered 2026-07-23 while working lifeos PR #100 (blueprint reconciliation). Running `bun run test` mutates 5 tracked generated files under planning-spine-v0/, exposing three distinct defects.

REPRO
  git checkout -- .        # clean tree
  bun run test             # 509/510; also silently rewrites tracked files
  git status               # 5 planning-spine-v0/* files now dirty

FINDING 1 — tests write tracked artifacts (bypass-shaped)
The navigation generator (planning-spine-v0/scripts/build-navigation-index.mjs --write) and the verifier authority run DURING the test suite and overwrite committed files. "Green" then partly means "the suite overwrote the baseline," which is the untrusted pattern. Tests must call the --check variants (planning-spine:navigation:check), never --write.

FINDING 2 — non-deterministic timestamps in tracked reports
planning-spine-v0/state/authority_integrity_report.json and state/mvp_bundle_report.json embed a live observed_at wall-clock (e.g. 2026-07-22T16:00:00Z -> 2026-07-23T10:30:24Z). Result: permanent phantom-dirty after any run, training everyone to `git checkout --` them without looking. Freeze/remove observed_at from tracked output, or gitignore these reports.

FINDING 3 — generated nav is stale vs source AND coupled to the whole dirty corpus
Committed navigation_graph.json / navigation_index.json / navigation.validation_report.json were stale vs source on 2 nodes: CLAUDE.md (gained an ICM section) and 1.0_VISION/Architecture_Anchors/section_inventory.json (-54B). Worse, regen output — including inventory_sha256 and per-node byte_counts — is a function of the ENTIRE inventoried corpus, including uncommitted working-tree files. Evidence: inventory_sha256 took 3 distinct values across runs (committed dea29af4 / docs-branch 242404e8 / base-branch ae5378b1) purely from tree state; CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and section_inventory.json are all currently dirty. So an ISOLATED nav regen bakes transient/foreign uncommitted state and re-stales the instant those edits land. Nav must be regenerated as part of (or gated on) the commit that changes any inventoried source.

RECOMMENDED FIX
1. Test suite invokes planning-spine:navigation:check (and the other :check gates), not :write.
2. Drop live timestamps from tracked reports, or gitignore state/*_report.json.
3. Add a pre-commit + CI gate running `bun run planning-spine:navigation:check` (+ anchors/consolidation checks) so source and generated stay coupled.
4. The actual nav resync should ride the commit that lands the pending CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md / section_inventory.json edits — not a standalone commit.

RELATED: lifeos PR #100 (docs/blueprint-gap-audit-2026-07-23).

title: planning-spine anchors importer reads blueprint from hardcoded ~/Downloads absolute path (non-reproducible, breaks CI)
description:
planning-spine-v0/scripts/import-architecture-anchors.mjs (readAndVerify, ~line 52 / expectedArtifacts ~line 151) reads the architecture blueprint from a HARDCODED ABSOLUTE PATH: /home/flexnetos/Downloads/Architecture_Data_Pipeline_Blueprint_RUVECTOR_FULLY_EXPANDED_VERIFIED (1).md

IMPACT
- `bun run planning-spine:anchors:check` and `--write` throw "missing architecture anchor: /home/flexnetos/Downloads/...(1).md" whenever that file is absent — i.e. ALWAYS on a GitHub-hosted runner, and currently even locally. This is a hard CI blocker on `bun run check` independent of the systemd-harness test failures.
- section_inventory.json therefore cannot be regenerated deterministically; the generated planning-spine anchor state is non-reproducible and pinned to one machine`s Downloads folder.

WHY IT MATTERS
The architecture blueprint is the repo`s normative authority and a committed file (Architecture_Data_Pipeline_Blueprint_RUVECTOR_FULLY_EXPANDED_VERIFIED.md at repo root). The importer must read the IN-REPO copy, not an external Downloads path.

FIX
- Point the importer at the repo-root blueprint (resolve relative to repo root), or make the source path a documented arg/env with the repo copy as default.
- Then regenerate section_inventory.json + receipts deterministically and commit alongside any blueprint edit (see related drift incident lifeos-spine-generated-state-drift-pau).

DISCOVERED 2026-07-23 while attempting the nav/anchor resync for lifeos PR #100.

title: gha-runner: metaharness host cannot run arbitrary GitHub workflows (design gap vs requirement)
description:
Surfaced by /verify on yazelix PR #109 (feat/flexnetos-gha-runner-nix-2026-07-23). Requirement: "the runner must run ALL github workflows/actions". The shipped design uses @metaharness/host-github-actions, which is an AGENT-HARNESS HOST — it runs agentic harness tasks AS a GHA job (ADR-033), NOT a general self-hosted runner that executes arbitrary workflows like `bun run check`. So it structurally cannot meet the requirement, and cannot run the FlexNetOS-env archbp-* tests that motivated it.

Also checked the user-referenced OSSystems/nix-actions: its README states it does NOT provide a self-hosted runner — it is two composite actions (nix flake check + flake.lock bump) that run as steps ON an existing runner. Does not fill the gap.

FIX: re-base the runner on the standard self-hosted actions/runner (nixpkgs `github-runner`) inside the hermetic flake (zero-OS-deps preserved by running from the nix closure), with `runs-on: [self-hosted, flexnetos, nix]`. Keep metaharness/agentic-flow as OPTIONAL orchestration invoked BY workflows, not as the runner itself. Then prove by dispatching a workflow and reading the run.
Related: yazelix PR #109; unblocks lifeos CI (archbp-* on GitHub-hosted runners).
notes: Composed design landed: yazelix e53cfe1 — pkgs.github-runner substrate (nixpkgs 2.334.0) + metaharness agent layer in one flake; smoke workflow targets [self-hosted,flexnetos,nix]. Live registration + dispatch proof pending substrate source build.
close_reason: PROVEN: run 30014284241 ran on flexnetos-nix [self-hosted,flexnetos,nix], executed actions/checkout@v4 + all steps green — substrate runs arbitrary workflows/actions. yazelix 859ce4a.

title: gha-runner: runner.nu crashes on line 1 — Nushell string-interpolation subexpression bug
description:
Surfaced by /verify (yazelix PR #109). Driving `nu scripts/runner.nu doctor` crashes immediately, exit 1:
  x Command `path-law:` not found
  34 | print $"[flexnetos-runner] work-dir: ($wd)  (path-law: profile-runtime, not ~/.local)"
Nushell parses the parenthesized `(path-law: ...)` inside the string interpolation as a SUBEXPRESSION command call. The launcher never reaches its first real action; it never checks the token or a harness.

ROOT CAUSE OF THE MISS: last turn claimed "runner.nu parses" — but `nu -c "source runner.nu"` only sources the def, it does not RUN `main`. Parse != run.

FIX: escape/restructure the interpolation so parenthesized prose is not evaluated (e.g. drop the parens, or build the string without a bare `(word: ...)`). ADD a real smoke gate to verify.mjs / CI that actually RUNS `nu scripts/runner.nu doctor` and asserts exit behavior, not just parse.
notes: runner.nu rewritten (yazelix e53cfe1): no parentheticals inside string interpolation; nu-check passes; runtime doctor proof pending.
close_reason: PROVEN: launcher doctor/register/run ran clean end-to-end in the live proof; nu-check green. yazelix 859ce4a.

title: gha-runner: runner is unbuilt/unscaffolded — no bin/cli.js, npmDepsHash=fakeHash
description:
Surfaced by /verify (yazelix PR #109). harness/ contains only package.json — there is NO bin/cli.js (the plain-ESM harness entrypoint is generated by `nix run .#scaffold`, never run). And flake.nix sets `npmDepsHash = lib.fakeHash`, so `nix build .#metaharness` fails until the real hash is pasted. Net: nothing is built, nothing runs, so no "test metrics to prove worth" are producible.

FIX (sequenced after the design-gap issue is resolved): (1) generate the harness bin/cli.js + package-lock via the scaffold step; (2) compute npmDepsHash from the first `nix build` and pin it; (3) commit the lockfile so the build is reproducible. If the design re-bases on github-runner, this scaffold step changes accordingly.
Related: yazelix PR #109.
close_reason: PROVEN: real metaharness scaffold + real npmDepsHash sha256-oOFpGJ...; hermetic build; agent-layer doctor passed ON the substrate in run 30014284241.

title: Restore Nix binary substituters (cache.nixos.org + nix-community) — strict-local-build policy disables them
description: DISCOVERED during the gha-runner build (2026-07-23). The profile-owned yazelix_host_policy (yazelix nushell/system/host_policy.nu) installs /etc/nix/nix.conf + nix.custom.conf with 'substitute = false', empty substituters, empty trusted-public-keys — a deliberate 'strict local-build policy' ('Kache is the only persistent compilation cache; Nix substitutes are disabled'). Effect: nix builds EVERYTHING from source (e.g. github-runner drags dotnet-vmr = multi-hour from-source compile) even though cache.nixos.org has the signed prebuilt (proven: override → 70 paths/103MiB fetched in seconds vs 1212 source derivations). Kache is a COMPILE cache (object files), NOT a store-path substituter — it does not cover cold heavyweight closures. Owner directive: restore binary caches; keep Kache where it fits; require-sigs stays true (signed-only). FIX (atomic, needs profile rebuild): (1) edit source templates nix.conf + nix.custom.conf → substitute=true, always-allow-substitutes=true, substituters='https://cache.nixos.org https://nix-community.cachix.org', trusted-public-keys='cache.nixos.org-1:6NCHdD59X431o0gWypbMrAURkbJ16ZPMQFGspcDShjY= nix-community.cachix.org-1:mB9FSh9qf2dCimDSUo8Zy7bkq5CX+/rkCWyvRCYg3Fs='; (2) update host_policy.nu verification gates (lines ~88-92 substring checks; ~167-174 nix config show expected values) to match; (3) profile rebuild to apply to /etc/nix; (4) verify: nix config show substituters non-empty + a dry-run of github-runner shows 'will be fetched'. Immediate unblock already in use: trusted-user (flexnetos) per-command override --option substitute true --option substituters ... --option trusted-public-keys ... (reversible, no root).

title: Fix GitKB production document embedding timeout
description:
## Reproduction

1. Start the Meta GitKB daemon.
2. Set `[embeddings] model = "sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2"`.
3. Run `git-kb ai embed --scope documents --force`.

The canonical Meta store reports 463 eligible documents but the daemon IPC request times out after 30 seconds and persists zero document vectors. `GITKB_DAEMON_TIMEOUT=120` does not alter this request timeout.

## Evidence

- Default BGE Candle model fails: `index-select invalid index 512 with dim size 512`.
- MiniLM succeeds in a fresh 221-document fixture: 457 document vectors and semantic invocation.
- Production Meta generates code vectors but zero document vectors.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Production document embedding completes for the canonical Meta KB.
- [ ] `git-kb ai embed stats` reports non-zero document vectors.
- [ ] `git-kb ai semantic "PostgreSQL RuVector ingress" --scope documents --json` executes successfully.
- [ ] Daemon/client timeout is configurable or batch work is made resumable below the IPC deadline.

Related KB task: `tasks/meta-gitkb-daemon-embedding-proof-suite`; Meta Git commit: `10d9468`.

title: Restore uv Python build cache for notebooklm-py + hermes-agent
description:
Repos touched: src/notebooklm-py, src/hermes-agent (workspace uv Python repos).

WORK: The Python uv cache (uv cache dir = /run/user/1001/yazelix/volatile/cache/uv) was empty. Ran uv sync in both repos, populating the shared uv cache to 67M and creating .venv (gitignored) in each. This was the only genuinely-missing build cache in the 45-repo meta-workspace: kache 0.10.0 (Rust) was already installed+wired+warm (kache doctor PASS, 45GiB store, hit-rate rose 30.9%->59.3% intra-session); bun install cache already warm (110M); no repo has turbo.json so Turborepo is inapplicable; did NOT run a full cold build (kache store 90% full -> LRU eviction hazard) and did NOT run kache init (profile owns ~/.cargo/config.toml, Path Law).

VERIFICATION (offline, cache-only): rm -rf .venv && uv sync --offline -> exit 0 both repos; uv run --offline notebooklm --help and hermes --help -> exit 0 both. Proves the restored cache serves deps with no network.

FOOTPRINT: zero trackable git changes (both repos git status --porcelain=0; .venv is gitignored; uv.lock/pyproject clean). Nothing committable -> this beads issue is the tracking record.

RESIDUAL GAP (owner-gated, not fixed): uv cache lives on volatile tmpfs -> clears on reboot and forces copy-not-hardlink; durable fix is the envctl/XDG migration (relocate to meta/var/xdg-data/uv), which a running session structurally cannot perform.
close_reason: Fixed: uv cache restored to 67M; verified offline (uv sync --offline exit 0 + notebooklm/hermes --help offline exit 0) in both notebooklm-py and hermes-agent.
